draft.title: ai workspace
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
ai workspace

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: The user may prefer organic, approachable forms over stark geometry in their AI workspace
facade.label: Soft rounded corners
facade.acceptImplies: Lean toward human-friendly, tactile aesthetic; curved UI elements, softer edges, less intimidating interface
facade.rejectImplies: Lean toward clean, precise, technical aesthetic; sharp corners, grid-based layout, minimal ornamentation

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer smooth, continuous visual feedback over instant state changes when interacting with workspace elements
facade.axisTargeted: Animation presence: continuous feedback vs discrete jumps
facade.label: Subtle hover glow
facade.acceptImplies: AI workspace should use layered micro-animations (glow, fade, blur shifts) to signal interactivity and system responsiveness
facade.rejectImplies: AI workspace should favor instant visual updates and minimal animation to prioritize speed and clarity of state change

evidence.hypothesis: The user may prefer organic, approachable forms over stark geometry in their AI workspace
evidence.implication: Lean toward human-friendly, tactile aesthetic; curved UI elements, softer edges, less intimidating interface

facade.hypothesis: Users working on AI projects want to compare outputs, prompts, or results visually across columns rather than scrolling through sequential text.
facade.axisTargeted: Information layout: parallel columnar view vs. linear narrative flow
facade.label: Side-by-side comparison
facade.acceptImplies: The workspace should prioritize split-screen or multi-column layouts for comparing AI outputs, code, or data side-by-side
facade.rejectImplies: The workspace should favor sequential, full-width displays where comparisons happen through scrolling, tabs, or modal dialogs rather than simultaneous column layouts

facade.hypothesis: AI workspace users prefer a fixed left navigation with card-based content areas over top tabs with list rows
facade.axisTargeted: navigation structure (sidebar vs tabs) combined with content container shape (cards vs lists)
facade.label: Sidebar + cards
facade.acceptImplies: Explore denser card layouts, collapsible sidebar states, and card-based filtering UI
facade.rejectImplies: Test horizontal tab navigation and list-based content; consider whether tabs feel more lightweight or if lists better suit data-heavy workspaces

facade.hypothesis: The AI workspace voice should be friendly and playful rather than formal and corporate
facade.axisTargeted: friendly vs professional + playful vs serious
facade.label: Casual and witty
facade.acceptImplies: User prefers conversational, humorous tone with personality in UI copy and interactions
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers straightforward, business-like communication without jokes or casual language

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal, clean interfaces vs. rich visual detail and color
Whether user wants tools always visible vs. hidden/revealed on demand
Whether user responds to encouraging, conversational guidance vs. neutral, direct instruction
Whether user seeks calm, distraction-free focus vs. energetic, stimulating environment
Whether user prefers flat, simple information hierarchy vs. layered, detailed organization
Whether user wants instant feedback and snappy responses vs. smooth, considered transitions

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimal, clean interfaces vs. rich visual detail and color
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants tools always visible vs. hidden/revealed on demand
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to encouraging, conversational guidance vs. neutral, direct instruction
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user seeks calm, distraction-free focus vs. energetic, stimulating environment
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers flat, simple information hierarchy vs. layered, detailed organization
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants instant feedback and snappy responses vs. smooth, considered transitions
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: The user may prefer restrained, spacious text treatment over information-dense layouts in their AI workspace
facade.label: Minimal typography
facade.acceptImplies: Lean toward generous whitespace around text, larger font sizes, fewer visual competing elements—reducing cognitive load in interface
facade.rejectImplies: User may tolerate or prefer denser information hierarchy, smaller type, more compact layouts—suggesting a busier, more feature-forward workspace aesthetic

facade.hypothesis: Users working in AI tools prefer high contrast between navigation and content areas to reduce cognitive load during long focus sessions
facade.label: Dark sidebar, bright canvas
facade.acceptImplies: Explore dark UI frames with luminous work surfaces; test sidebar-driven layouts
facade.rejectImplies: Move toward unified tone (all-light or all-dark); explore canvas-first designs without heavy framing

draft.title: AI Workspace Hub
draft.summary: A focused workspace interface for managing AI tasks, projects, and collaborative workflows. Clean, minimal design with warm neutrals and intuitive navigation.
